title: Container Packaging, Startup, Health, Migration Coordination, and Rollout · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs container packaging, startup, health, migration coordination, and rollout from source and emitted revision, tenant, URL, validator, bytes, runtime validation, Promise settlement, transaction, lease, response, failure, handle cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for deployable emitted TypeScript feed service after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Inspect packed ESM exports, emitted JavaScript, maps, production dependencies, migrations, licenses, and startup command in a clean Node 24 environment.
Build a pinned non-root minimal image with exact package, certificates, ownership, metadata, SBOM, signature, and vulnerability evidence.
Validate configuration, dependency compatibility, migration state, secrets, budgets, and separate startup, liveness, readiness, degradation, and maintenance semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

stimulus.title: Feed Ts Progressive Release case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review TypeScript team handles case 8970 during an out-of-order Promise and AbortSignal rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bind package, non-root container, health, migration ownership, compatibility, canary, and rollback evidence; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant work must preserve the prior admitted state; then reorder one timer, Promise, claim, lease, fencing, worker, disconnect, or shutdown settlement and exclude stale effects. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Canary source, package, schema, parser, sanitizer, worker, and API versions with SLO, capacity, kill-switch, rollback, and handle evidence.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A successful container smoke justifies full rollout.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Canary source, package, schema, parser, sanitizer, worker, and API versions with SLO, capacity, kill-switch, rollback, and handle evidence. Artifact under review: deployable emitted TypeScript feed service.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Feed Ts Progressive Release, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, cast one convenient XML object as a feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, source and emitted revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, entity and dangerous-key limits, unknown runtime validation, Fetch stream and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL client, Promise settlement, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as feed-service proof.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Canary source, package, schema, parser, sanitizer, worker, and API versions with SLO, capacity, kill-switch, rollback, and handle evidence.
